text: The surgeon uses specified port sites and sizes to access the narrow male pelvis. A medial to lateral approach is employed to dissect the retrorectal space, separating the presacral fascia from the mesorectal fascia.

The surgeon identifies and preserves the hypogastric nerve while continuing dissection cranially. The inferior mesenteric artery is located at its origin, and the inferior mesenteric vein is identified below the pancreas, skeletonized, and divided between laparoscopic clips. The inferior mesenteric artery is then skeletonized one centimeter from its origin and divided using a 45mm white load stapler.

The surgeon continues the medial to lateral approach, carefully identifying and preserving the hypogastric nerves and the left ureter. The dissection progresses laterally, maintaining clear visibility of these anatomical structures.

The left ureter is visualized. Lateral dissection along the white line of Toldt is performed to mobilize the sigmoid and descending colon. The surgeon divides the gastrocolic and splenic ligaments to enter the omental bursa and mobilize the splenic flexure.

The surgeon fully mobilizes the left-sided colon and returns to the pelvis to perform total mesorectal excision. Branches of the inferior hypogastric nerves are identified and preserved. Anterior dissection is carried out along Denonvilliers fascia, using artisanal forceps to hold down the rectum, allowing precise dissection along the nervous fascia with an articulated spatula.

The surgeon continues posterior dissection, using articulated forceps to hold up the rectum and provide a clear view of the retrorectal space. The monopolar spatula is articulated upwards and sideways to dissect in the plane between the mesorectum and the coccygeal and levator ani muscles. Circumferential dissection proceeds laterally until all planes are connected, completing the total mesorectal excision.

The surgeon transects the rectum using a 45mm green load stapler in a narrow pelvis where the nerves are preserved. The transabdominal specimen is extracted, and extraabdominal fluorescence angiography is performed to assess bowel perfusion. A double stapled side-to-end anastomosis is created using a 29mm circular stapler.
